back_translation: As for Saul, he also consented to this killing of Stephen. On that very day a great persecution arose against the church in Jerusalem, and everyone except the apostles were scattered throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria.
There was a group of men who feared God who carried the body of Stephen away to bury it, and wailed and lamented heavily for him.
As for Saul, he began to destroy the church by forcibly entering houses one by one, dragging both men and women out and throwing them into prison.
As for those who had been scattered, they traveled throughout and proclaimed the word.
But as for Philip, he went down to a city in the region of Samaria and proclaimed concerning Christ to the people of the city.
The crowds in that city together listened intently to what Philip said with one mind, because they heard and saw the various signs that he did.
For unclean spirits that possessed many people cried out with a loud voice and came out, and many people who had paralysis and lame people were healed back to normal.
So there arose a great joy in that city.
But before that there was a certain man named Simon who lived in that city. He practiced magic and astounded the Samaritans, claiming himself to be someone great.
All the people, from the lesser to the greater, gave attention to him, saying, "This man is 'the Power of God' called 'the Great Power.'"
The reason they paid attention to him that way was because he had astounded them with magic for a long time.
But when they believed Philip, who was proclaiming the good news about the kingdom of God and the name of Jesus Christ, there was baptism-receiving, both men and women.
Even Simon himself also believed, and having received baptism he followed Philip closely, and when he saw the signs and great miracles that happened, he was astounded.
As for the apostles in Jerusalem, when they heard that the Samaritans had received the word of God, they sent Peter and John down to them.
When the two had gone down and arrived, they prayed on behalf of them so that they would receive the Holy Spirit.
Because the Holy Spirit had not yet come down upon any of them at all — they had only received baptism in the name of the Lord Jesus.
Then the two laid their hands on those people, and they received the Holy Spirit.
When Simon saw that the Spirit was bestowed through the laying on of hands of the apostles, he brought money to offer to them.
Saying, "Please bestow this authority to me also, so that whoever I lay my hands on, that person will receive the Holy Spirit."
Peter then answered him, "Let your money perish along with you! Because you thought you could buy the gift from God with money in that way."
You have no part or any inheritance in this matter, because your heart is not upright toward God.
Therefore repent decisively from this wickedness of yours, and pray to the Lord, so that he may forgive you of that thought in your heart.
Because I see that you have fallen into bitterness that is like poison, and are in captivity of unrighteousness."
Simon then answered, "Please, both of you, pray to the Lord on my behalf also, so that anything you have said may not happen to me at all."
As for Peter and John, when they had testified and spoken the word of the Lord, they traveled back to Jerusalem, preaching the gospel along many villages of the Samaritans.
At that time, an angel of the Lord spoke to Philip, saying, "Arise and travel to the south, to the road that goes down from Jerusalem to the city of Gaza." (That road passes through a wilderness.)

Philip then rose up and set out on the journey. And behold, there was a certain Ethiopian, a eunuch who was a high-ranking official of Kandake, the queen of the Ethiopians, who was the caretaker of all her royal treasury, and had gone up to worship at Jerusalem.
At that moment, he was traveling back, sitting on his carriage, reading the book of Isaiah the prophet.
The Spirit then spoke to Philip, "Go near and stay close to that carriage."
Philip ran to approach, and heard him reading the book of Isaiah the prophet, and asked, "Do you understand what you are reading or not?"
He answered, "How could I understand, if no one guides me?" Then he invited Philip to come up and sit together with him.

The passage in the Scripture that he was reading was this: 'He was led like a lamb being led to be killed, and like a lamb silent and still before the one who shears its wool, he did not open his mouth at all,
in his humiliation, justice was plundered away from him. Who then can describe his descendants? Because his life has been taken away from the world.'
The eunuch then answered Philip, "Please, sir, tell me, whom is the prophet speaking about? About himself or about another person?"
Philip then opened his mouth, beginning from that Scripture passage, and proclaimed the gospel concerning Jesus to him.
While they were traveling along that road, they came to a water source. The eunuch then said, "Look, here is water! Is there anything preventing me from receiving baptism?"
He therefore ordered the carriage to stop. Then the two went down into the water, both Philip and the eunuch. And Philip performed the baptism rite for him.
When the two came up from the water, the Spirit of the Lord took Philip away. The eunuch did not see him again at all, but continued his journey with joy.
As for Philip, he appeared at the city of Azotus, and traveled through proclaiming the gospel in various cities, until he reached the city of Caesarea.
